name: Condimento Ai Funghi (Wild Mushroom Topping)
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Fresh cremini or portobello mushrooms
1 lb Ripe tomatoes, peeled, seeded & finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the porcini mushrooms in 1 1/2 cups warm water for at least 30 minutes.
2. Drain the mushrooms in a sieve lined with cheesecloth, reserving the liquid for use elsewhere.
3. Squeeze the porcini and wash them in fresh water.
4. Cut the clean fresh mushrooms into very fine pieces.
5. Place the mushroom pieces in a medium-sized bowl.
6. Add the drained porcini, tomatoes, rosemary, sage, garlic, and pepper to the bowl.
7. Mix together well.
8. Toss with the lemon juice and olive oil.
9. Cover and leave for 24 hours.
10. Just before serving, add the salt. If it seems too moist, drain off any extra moisture.
11. Use as an appetizer salad or as a topping for grilled bread. Or omit the lemon and use it as a pasta sauce.
